creator: gour@atmarama.net
text:
I experience that my OS is getting too hot in comparison with e.g. Debian (Sid) which I still have installed on my 2nd identical HD.

In the Linux in idle state there is maximum of 49C, while the same kind of 'work' in Free/PC-BSD produces >65C.

The main problem is that with such system is not suitable for compiling or building any port from the source since very soon my system is getting at >90C and finnally it shuts down after ariving at 99C.

Compiling same application (e.g. Julia language) under Linux never went over 55C.

At the moment, just copying files to external USB HD (I'm cp-ing data back to Linux) brings OS to 70C.

I did write about my experience in this thread: http://forums.pcbsd.org/showthread.php?t=22388

and asked for help in #freebsd.

powerd setting from my rc.conf:


# powerd stuff
powerd_flags="-a adaptive" 
performance_cx_lowest="C2"
economy_cx_lowest="C2"

Originally I had only "-a hiadaptive" and those new settings did not improve anything.

I was happy trying Free/PC-BSD, but simply have no time fiddling too much with the system with stuff which simply works under Debian (I was hoping to have *less* admin work with Free/PC-BSD) and therefore going back to Debian (Sid), but according to the hint from PC-BSD forums, submitting this PR if it can be of some help for someone in the future.

In the attachment I include output of 'sysctl -a' stripped of some stuff hopefully not relevant for this problem

Sincerely,
Gour

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
